patterns_recognized: Parse-error prevention: model_pick MUST come from the CSV `pick` column, verified by sign of full_prob vs 0.5. Prior runs (5/29) incorrectly wrote MIL@HOU as MIL and ARI@SEA as ARI; today's file fixes both.

D-grade dual-opposing-signal (SP xERA AND PQI both against the pick) shows ~70-75% directional failure rate per 5/20, 5/28, 5/29 archives. TOR@BAL (Yesavage 2.27 xERA edge + PQI Δ=-26.9) qualifies; DOWNGRADE enforced.

Negative market edge (edge_pp < 0) should not be CONFIRM-rescued even when barely negative. HARD CAP 1 intent is to defer to market pricing. CHC@STL (-3.56pp), ATL@CIN (-0.98pp), ARI@SEA (-3.31pp) all DNQ for rescue.

Extreme f5_full_delta (>0.20) with near-zero hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap cannot support bullpen-carry thesis. CHC@STL (delta=0.32, gap=-0.003) and PHI@LAD (delta=0.33, gap=-0.009) are classic unsupported-carry patterns.

NYY@OAK PLATINUM (score=7, grade=A, edge=+11.55pp, kelly=22.9%) is the only live bet today — second consecutive game NYY@OAK after an 8-2 NYY blowout yesterday. Clean PLATINUM with no hard caps fired.

DET @ CHW.reasoning: D-grade (-3) with dual-opposing signals: Framber Valdez (DET away SP) holds 1.29 xERA edge over Anthony Kay, and PQI Δ=-16.2 opposes CHW's late-game profile. No market data (fair_prob missing). CHW's 70.1% full-game probability appears to be a calibration artifact — the underlying signals favor DET. Both primary grade signals oppose the CHW pick. Zero-stake is correctly enforced; no qualitative reason to override.

SD @ WSH.reasoning: D-grade (-2) with dual-opposing signals: Michael King (SD away SP) holds 0.51 xERA edge over Foster Griffin (WSH home SP), and PQI Δ=-11.0 opposes WSH's late-game profile. No fair_prob available for market validation. The BvP data for SD batters vs Foster Griffin shows suspicious identical stats (all 5 batters: 10 PA, 1.0 OPS, shrunk 0.790) — likely a data artifact, not reliable BvP evidence. SKIP confirmed; directional confidence in WSH is low.

KC @ TEX.reasoning: Pre-cap score=5 (A) with F1*+F3 confirming TEX, SP edge 0.61 xERA, PQI +7.9, Stage agree. HARD CAP 7 fires (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0019 ≥ 0, score 5→1) and fair_prob missing caps further to C (score 0). Joc Pederson is WEAK_VS Seth Lugo (20 PA, shrunk 0.597) — one meaningful BvP concern for TEX's cleanup hitter. F1_xera_gap=0.79* (thin sample) limits xERA conviction. CONFIRM the zero-stake; the directional case is sound but the caps are authoritative.

TOR @ BAL.reasoning: D-grade (-1) with dual-opposing signals: Brandon Young (TOR away SP) holds 2.27 xERA edge over Trey Yesavage, and PQI Δ=-26.9 opposes BAL. The F3_swing_take_gap=4190.7 fires (+1) but cannot overcome dual SP and PQI opposition. BAL was similarly D-grade when TOR won 2-1 in the 5/28 matchup. pick_prob=50.77% (extreme bottom-bucket). TOR top-3 BvP vs Yesavage shows suspicious identical stats (all 15 PA, 1.267 OPS), likely a data artifact. Downgrade to SKIP; no qualitative basis to rescue a D-grade 50.8% pick.

MIN @ PIT.reasoning: Pre-cap score=0 (before any hard caps): F3 fires (+1), SP edge 0.47 xERA against PIT (-2 would apply, but net grade_score=0 reflects Stage agree +1 offsetting). HARD CAP 10 fired on the shadow model's ~0.526 probability reading. Josh Bell is an OWNER vs Bailey Ober (18 PA, shrunk 0.926) — a meaningful BvP concern for PIT allowing scoring. However, PIT's pre-cap score was already 0; my CONFIRM has no rescuing effect here. Zero-stake confirmed.

BOS @ CLE.reasoning: D-grade (-3) with all primary signals against BOS: Sonny Gray (CLE home SP) holds 1.22 xERA edge over Parker Messick, PQI Δ=+14.1 opposes BOS's late-game profile. José Ramírez has LOTS_OF_HISTORY vs Messick (48 PA, shrunk 0.622) — his historical struggles against Messick suggest CLE's cleanup bat is neutralized, but this is a CLE advantage (Ramírez is WEAK_VS Messick, bad for CLE's biggest threat). BOS wins at only 50.46%. D-grade SKIP confirmed; no override case.

LAA @ TB.reasoning: Grade C (score=0) despite grade_reasons showing only Stage agree (+1) and team quality disabled. edge_pp=57.07pp (HARD CAP 6 territory) with no fair_prob anchor makes the edge unreliable. HARD CAP 10 officially reduced score to zero. BvP is a wash: Yandy Diaz OWNER (11 PA, shrunk 0.812) for TB vs Detmers, Adam Frazier OWNER (13 PA, shrunk 0.793) for LAA vs Rasmussen — one OWNER each side. f5_full_delta=0.1781 with hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.002 (near-zero) cannot support the full-game thesis. CONFIRM zero-stake.

MIA @ NYM.reasoning: Model picks MIA (away) at 64.76% full-game probability with strong pre-cap signals: F3_swing_take_gap=3264.2, SP edge 0.87 xERA agrees (Phillips over Scott), PQI Δ=-11.3 confirms. However, fair_prob is missing — Odds API did not fire — which caps the grade at C regardless of signal strength. edge_pp=63.76pp is in HARD CAP 6 hallucination territory and unreliable without fair_prob. BvP data shows all batters at uniform shrunk_OPS (~0.568 MIA vs ~0.635 NYM), likely data artifacts. CONFIRM zero-stake; MIA direction is qualitatively sound but unstakeable without market pricing.

MIL @ HOU.reasoning: HOU picked at only 50.76% probability — extreme bottom-bucket. Pre-cap score=5 (A) reflects strong signals (F1*+F3, SP edge 1.05, PQI +9.3 confirming HOU), but if I CONFIRM, HARD CAP 10 doesn't fire and the score jumps to 5 (A-grade) for a 50.8% coin-flip — clearly inappropriate. MIL won 5-4 road at HOU yesterday (5/29 postgame confirms). CONFIRM-rescued picks in (0.50, 0.55] hit only 47.6% historically (below 50% breakeven). F1_xera_gap=1.05* is thin-sample. Downgrade to SKIP; no qualitative basis to stake a near-50-50 game.

CHC @ STL.reasoning: F1_xera_gap=4.09 (full-sample, no asterisk — Ben Brown over Leahy by 4.09 xERA) is the strongest SP edge on today's slate, and PQI Δ=-40.1 strongly confirms CHC. Despite this, DOWNGRADE is correct: edge_pp=-3.56pp (negative, market prices CHC at 55.53% vs model's 51.97% — we'd be betting at LOWER conviction than market). The massive f5_full_delta=0.3221 requires STL bullpen to erase CHC's F5 dominance (STL wins full game 48% vs CHC wins F5 84%+), but hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0023 (near-zero) cannot support a 32pp swing. HARD CAP 1's intent is clear: negative-edge picks should not be staked.

ATL @ CIN.reasoning: ATL has legitimate qualitative support: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0267 (ATL bullpen meaningfully better), pen_strain_pick_side=0.1171 (HIGH above 0.115), PQI Δ=-32.8 (strongly confirms ATL). Matt Olson OWNER vs Singer (15 PA, shrunk 0.913). However, edge_pp=-0.98pp (market gives ATL 54.83%, model says 53.86% — market more confident in ATL than our model), and confidence_downgrade=True. HARD CAP 1 intent covers even barely-negative edges: we should defer to the market that already prices this correctly. F1_xera_gap=2.28* (thin-sample Pérez). ATL won yesterday 8-3; today is game 2 with different pitchers but same principle holds: don't bet into negative edge.

SF @ COL.reasoning: Pre-cap score=1 (B-) with positive edge=7.85pp (model: 56.1%, market: 48.23%). Ordinarily this positive edge would be worth examining, but acute_roster=True flags an unknown roster issue that could undermine COL's projection, and confidence_downgrade=True adds pipeline caution. Coors Field altitude reduces SP-edge signal reliability for COL home picks (5/29 postgame: COL won 8-6 despite D-grade with Webb xERA 1.32 opposing — Coors altitude creates variance the model doesn't fully capture). Willy Adames is WEAK_VS Feltner (12 PA, shrunk 0.588) — SF's leadoff has historically struggled here. DOWNGRADE due to acute_roster uncertainty; the edge may not reflect ground truth.

NYY @ OAK.reasoning: The only live bet on today's slate. Score=7 (A grade), no hard caps fired. NYY supported by F2_xwoba_gap=0.024, F5_bullpen_gap=+0.44, PQI Δ=-3.5, edge=+11.55pp (valid [4,15] range), kelly=22.9%. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0145 (NYY pen better) and Stage 1/2 agree (Δ=0.07, no carry uncertainty). OAK batters show no OWNER threats vs Ginn (all SMALL_SAMPLE). NYY blew out OAK 8-2 yesterday (PLATINUM CONFIRM correct, 5/29 postgame). PLATINUM historical rate is 43% (below GOLD), but this is the cleanest signal stack in recent archive — every gate cleared, positive edge, real kelly. CONFIRM with appropriate PLATINUM caution.

ARI @ SEA.reasoning: Pre-cap score=4 (A-) with strong signals: F1_xera_gap=1.98 (no asterisk — Bryan Woo over Ryne Nelson), F3_swing_take_gap=2799.3, PQI Δ=+20.6 confirming SEA, SEA bullpen better (-0.0138). However, edge_pp=-3.31pp: market gives SEA 59.06% (fair_prob=0.5906) while model says 55.75%. We'd be betting SEA at lower confidence than market pricing. confidence_downgrade=True adds pipeline caution. BvP is fully empty (NO_DATA for both sides vs today's pitchers). HARD CAP 1 fires correctly; do not rescue. ARI was the model's D-grade SKIP yesterday and lost 6-7 to SEA — SEA direction is right but the model's edge vs market is inverted today.

PHI @ LAD.reasoning: D-grade (-4) — worst on the slate. All grade signals oppose LAD: Jesús Luzardo (PHI) holds 1.80 xERA edge over Roki Sasaki, PQI Δ=-29.0 opposes LAD, Stage 1/2 disagree (PHI wins F5 at 69.2% but LAD wins full game at 63.8% — f5_full_delta=0.3299). The carry thesis requires hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0085 to overcome a 33pp Stage delta — near-zero gap is structurally insufficient. LAD's lineup has 2 WEAK_VS vs Luzardo (Betts shrunk 0.605/14 PA, Tucker shrunk 0.566/17 PA) offsetting Freeman's OWNER (shrunk 0.868/11 PA). LAD won yesterday 4-2 (PLATINUM, 5/29 postgame), but today's D-grade context is entirely different. CONFIRM SKIP.
